purpose: Central hook wiring for Claude Code. Maps tool events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop) and tool matchers (Edit|Write, Task, Bash) to the executable hook scripts in .claude/hooks/. Wires 14 distinct hook invocations: PreToolUse on Edit|Write fires 6 hooks (dashboard isolation, tracker-root writes, master-write gate, plan-required, init immutability, init schema); PreToolUse on Task fires 2 (worker prompt validation, approval gate); PreToolUse on Bash fires 3 (dashboard isolation bash, tracker-root writes bash, archive-before-clear); PostToolUse on Write fires 5 (progress validation, log detail, mark stale, log entry, master state); PostToolUse on Edit|Write fires nudge-progress-update; Stop fires verify-final-report. This is the single source of truth for which hook runs when — the harness reads this file to know what to execute.
gotchas: Hooks listed under the same matcher run sequentially in declaration order — PreToolUse Edit|Write runs all 6 hooks for every Edit/Write call (even if the first one allows)
Hook commands are relative paths (`.claude/hooks/X.sh`) — Claude Code resolves them relative to project root; CWD must be tracker_root
PostToolUse hooks cannot block (the write already happened) — they emit `{message: ...}` warnings the agent sees as feedback
Adding a new hook requires both: chmod +x the .sh file AND wire it in this JSON; either alone has no effect
agent-level hooks in .claude/agents/*.md are ALSO declared (master-orchestrator.md re-declares validate-master-write/verify-final-report) — these are agent-scoped, this file is global
patterns: Matcher is a regex-like alternation: 'Edit|Write' matches both tools
Each event type (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop) is an array of {matcher, hooks[]} groups
conventions: Hook command paths are relative to project root with `.sh` extension
type field is always `command` (no other type used)
